summary: Postinstall fetches and executes payload from anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket
details: dist/bootstrap.js performs fetch() calls to https://pub-046c52795b9445cd9f5cc5cb21b9d59f.r2.dev — an anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket address with no relation to any declared publisher infrastructure — and references process.env in the same code path. R2 buckets at pub-<hex>.r2.dev are mutable, attacker-controlled hosting where the served bytes can be swapped at any time without any change to the published npm package. Fetching opaque content from such a host at install or load time and handing it to the runtime is a dropper pattern: the installer is executing whatever the bucket owner is serving at that moment, with no version pin, no hash verification, and no publisher attestation. The destination shape matches known payload-distribution infrastructure used by recent npm cluster compromises. Installing this package allows arbitrary code execution on the installer's machine sourced from a third-party anonymous host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
